_comment: Canonical price-data sources for Greek toll roads. Use this list when refreshing prices in tolls.js. Each entry includes the operator's official 2026 prices page; rerun a comparison after each January 1 increase.

_schema.operator: Operator name (matches `operator` field in tolls.js entries)
_schema.tolls_in_data: Toll IDs in tolls.js belonging to this operator
_schema.structure: Brief description of how the operator organizes their pricing tables
_schema.last_verified: ISO date when prices were last cross-checked against this source
_schema.notes: Anything quirky about parsing or interpreting their prices

_classification_rules._intro: Read this BEFORE adding new tolls or rerunning a price audit. The April 2026 audit had three classes of bug — these rules exist to prevent recurrence.

every_station_is_one_of_three_types.frontal: Mainline toll. Located on the main carriageway, charged to ALL through traffic in both directions (with rare 'one direction free' exceptions like the old Rio-Antirrio). Field: `type: 'frontal'`. Has `bypass_directions` (the parallel local-road escape route).
every_station_is_one_of_three_types.side: Ramp/lateral toll. Located on a single ramp at an interchange, charged ONLY to traffic that uses that ramp. The traveler pays the side toll OR the frontal of that zone, never both. Field: `type: 'side'`. Each booth at a multi-booth interchange is its OWN entry (one row per booth), each carrying the published station price. `direction` field = direction of TRAVEL ('northbound'/'southbound'/'eastbound'/'westbound'); `role` = 'entry' or 'exit'.
every_station_is_one_of_three_types.bridge_or_tunnel: Special. Single crossing toll. Field: `type: 'bridge'` or `type: 'tunnel'`.

common_misclassifications_to_watch: Egnatia mainline frontals near Thessaloniki (Analipsi, Moustheni, Asprovalta, Malgara, Evzoni) are tagged as A1 in tolls.js but officially A2 Egnatia. Don't 'correct' them naively — many routes reference them and the route-builder treats them as part of the A1 chain. Re-tagging requires updating routes.js too.

Pelasgia and Moschochori frontals are listed by BOTH Aegean Motorway and Kentriki Odos sources because the Kentriki section ends and Aegean section begins right at this seam. Officially they belong to Aegean Motorway. tolls.js currently tags them as Kentriki Odos — historical artifact, not necessarily wrong, but watch for double-counting if you ever try to dedupe by operator.

Olympia Odos has TWO physical frontals near Corinth (Isthmos at lat 37.92, and the never-existed mythical 'Zevgolatio frontal' that was deleted Apr 2026). Don't recreate it. Zevgolatio is a ramp toll with two booths (eastbound entry + eastbound exit, both at lat ~37.93). Westbound traffic at Zevgolatio is FREE.

Some operators publish prices for stations they don't operate. Cross-reference: a station's `operator` is determined by who issues the receipt, not who owns the road. When in doubt: the operator listed in the official price PDF/page IS the operator.

operator_to_highway_canonical_map._note: This is what the data SHOULD look like for full normalization. tolls.js currently has known deviations (see notes above). Use this when adding NEW entries.

fuzzy_name_matcher_pitfalls._intro: The April 2026 audit's matcher silently dropped 4+ side tolls because their names share roots with frontals. NEVER use containment-based fuzzy matching for the audit. Use exact-match against a manual alias table instead.

examples_of_bad_matches.Makrychori (side) [€1.30 cat2]: Was matched to a1_makrychori frontal [€3.00 cat2] because both contained 'Makrychori'. Result: side toll silently dropped from MISSING list, never made it into spreadsheet.
examples_of_bad_matches.Leptokarya (side) [€2.10 cat2]: Was matched to a1_leptokarya frontal [€3.80 cat2] for the same reason.
examples_of_bad_matches.Kileler (side) [€2.80 cat2]: Was matched to a1_moschochori frontal because the frontal's name_en is 'Moschochori / Kileler'.
examples_of_bad_matches.M. Monastiriou (side) [€2.20 cat2]: Was matched to olympia_rio frontal because both contained 'rio' as a substring.

fuzzy_name_matcher_pitfalls.rule: When auditing a new price page against tolls.js, treat side and frontal as DIFFERENT namespaces. A side toll named 'Makrychori (side)' should match ONLY against side toll IDs containing 'makrychori', never against the frontal at the same interchange. Maintain an explicit alias table for ambiguous cases.

side_toll_completeness_protocol._intro: When pulling a new price update, do this BEFORE assuming the spreadsheet is complete:
side_toll_completeness_protocol.step1: Open each operator's price page. Look for sections labeled 'Πλευρικός' / 'side' / 'rampa' / 'lateral' / 'παράπλευρος'.
side_toll_completeness_protocol.step2: Count: how many side toll stations does the operator list?
side_toll_completeness_protocol.step3: Count: how many side toll entries (grouped by station name, not booth) exist in tolls.js for this operator?
side_toll_completeness_protocol.step4: These numbers should match. If they don't, the missing ones need adding.

current_known_gaps.Aegean Motorway side tolls: M. Monastiriou €2.20, Kileler €2.80, Pyrgetos €2.00, Platamonas €0.80, Makrychori (Θ) €1.30, Leptokarya (πλευρ.) €2.10 — all listed on the Aegean Motorway price page, none in tolls.js as of 2026-04-27. Need coords first.
current_known_gaps.Nea Odos side tolls: 6+ lateral stations listed (Kapandriti, Malakasa, Oinofyta on A1; Gavrolimni, Messolonghi, Gorgomylos on A5) — coords needed.
current_known_gaps.Egnatia ramp tolls: 10+ ramp stations (Panagia, Kalamia, Lagkada, Profitis, Vagiochori, Galipsos/Orfani, Eleftheroupoli, Ag. Andreas, Aspra Chomata, Vafeika) — coords needed.
current_known_gaps.Olympia Odos ramp tolls: 11 ramp stations (Nea Peramos, Pachi, Ag. Theodori, Kiato-ramp, Derveni, Akrata, Kalavryta, Drepano, Kato Achaia, Amaliada — currently only Zevgolatio booths added.
current_known_gaps.Kentriki Odos side tolls: 6 lateral stations (Molos, Mpralos, Stylida, Anavra, Proastio, Kalampaka) — coords needed.
current_known_gaps.Moreas side tolls: 3 lateral stations (Paradisia, Arfara, Thouria) — coords needed.

checklist_for_next_yearly_refresh: 1. Visit each operator URL listed in `sources` and copy their FULL 2026 price tables (frontal AND side/ramp).
2. For each frontal in tolls.js, verify cat1-4 prices match. Update via the apply-price-updates.py script pattern (regex on cat lines).
3. Cross-check the side toll count per operator (see side_toll_completeness_protocol above). Add any missing ones.
5. Update homepage stat strings in index.html (Athens-Thessaloniki, Athens-Patras, Athens-Ioannina) — these are hardcoded, not dynamic.
6. Bump cache-busting query strings in index.html for any changed JS/CSS files.

sources.structure: Single table with 5 frontal (Μετωπικός) and 10 side (Πλευρικός) stations. Cat 1-4 columns. 24% VAT included. Prices effective 01 January 2026.
sources.notes: Section: Raches → Larissa → Kleidi Imathias. Pelasgia and Moschochori are tagged as Kentriki Odos in tolls.js but officially are Aegean Motorway. SIDE TOLLS STILL MISSING from this dataset (need coords): M. Monastiriou €2.20, Kileler €2.80, Pyrgetos €2.00, Platamonas €0.80, Makrychori-side €1.30, Leptokarya-side €2.10.

sources.structure: 18 mainline frontal (14 main + 4 vertical-axis) + 17 ramp stations. Cat 1-4 columns. As of January 2026, operator changed to GEK TERNA-Egis consortium. Prices effective 01 January 2026.
sources.notes: tolls.js currently mis-tags 5 entries as A1 (Malgara, Analipsi, Moustheni, Asprovalta, Evzoni) — they are A2 Egnatia per official source. Cat 2 height limit is 2.70m (different from other operators' 2.20m). Greek-language URL also works.

sources.structure: Two tables: A.Th.E. section (3 frontals + 5 lateral) and Ionia Odos (4 frontals + 5 lateral). Cat 1-4 columns. Lateral stations are direction-specific (entry one way / exit the other). Prices effective 01 January 2026.
sources.notes: Section: Metamorfosi (Athens) → Skarfia, plus Antirrio → Ioannina. agios_konstantinos in tolls.js is named 'Agia Triada' which actually belongs to Kentriki Odos — verify next refresh.

sources.structure: Two tables: 7 frontal (Μετωπικός) and 11 ramp stations. Ramp tolls labeled (P) = to Patras, (A) = to Athens. Cat 1-4. Prices effective 01 January 2026.
sources.notes: Section: Elefsina → Corinth → Patras → Pyrgos. The duplicate frontal misnamed 'Zevgolatio' (formerly olympia_isthmos) was deleted Apr 2026 — Zevgolatio is officially a ramp toll, represented by olympia_zevgolatio_east_* entries. No toll collectors at ramp stations; payment via auto machines or e-Pass only.

sources.structure: Two tables: A.Th.E. section (2 frontals + 3 lateral) and E65 (3 frontals + 4 lateral). Cat 1-4. Lateral stations are direction-specific. Prices effective 01 January 2026.
sources.notes: Section: Skarfia → Raches on A1, plus Lamia → Kalambaka on E65. Same parent group as Nea Odos (GEK TERNA).

sources.structure: Single table: 6 frontals + 3 side stations (Παραδείσια, Αρφαρών, Θουρίας). All charged in both directions. Cat 1-4.
sources.notes: Section: Corinth → Tripoli → Kalamata, plus Lefktro → Sparta branch. moreas_corinth in tolls.js is named 'Spathvounio / Corinth' = official 'Spathovouni'.

sources.structure: Single-crossing prices for 9 vehicle classes (motorbikes, cars, cars-with-disability, trucks 2/3/4/5+ axles, buses 3 size tiers). Discount products (Aller-Retour, 30-trip) on a separate page.
sources.notes: tolls.js maps Gefyra's 9 categories into 4-category schema as: cat1=motorcycles, cat2=cars, cat3=trucks 2-axle (proxy for light commercial), cat4=trucks 4+ axle. Bus tariffs not represented. Domain blocks crawler bots; verify via cross-reference (e.g. en.protothema.gr toll-summary articles in early January each year). 2026 prices announced 01 January 2026.

sources.structure: Underwater tunnel between Aktio (Lefkada) and Preveza. Single-direction toll. Pricing not yet verified in this iteration.
sources.notes: Unverified — need to fetch official site and confirm 2026 prices. Currently using placeholder values in tolls.js.

sources.structure: Single ring road with single-pass entry pricing. 6 vehicle categories (motorcycles €1.25, cars €2.55, vans €2.55, cars-with-trailer €2.55, trucks 2-3 axle €6.30, trucks 4+ €10.10). 2026 marks first index-linked price increase since opening.
sources.notes: tolls.js maps the 6 official categories into 4: cat1=motorbikes, cat2=cars, cat3=trucks 2-3 axle (€6.30), cat4=trucks 4+ axle (€10.10). 2026 prices: €1.25/€2.55/€6.30/€10.10. Operator changed from Attikes Diadromes to Nea Attiki Odos Paraxorisi A.E. in late 2024.

_secondary_sources.name: ProtoThema English — annual January toll roundup
_secondary_sources.use_case: When operator websites are slow to update or cloak pricing behind cookie walls. ProtoThema publishes a comprehensive multi-operator price summary every January after the annual increase.
_secondary_sources.notes: Very reliable for cross-checking. Fetch in early-to-mid January each year.

_secondary_sources.name: HelpPost.gr — Greek toll directory
_secondary_sources.notes: Aggregator. Not the primary source. Use only as a sanity check.

_secondary_sources.name: Tolls.eu
_secondary_sources.notes: Aggregator, may lag operator pricing by weeks.
